---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: bec0cc356c850631d552a5a35ef7e2f1b9202544
findings:
  - { id: judged-proposal-tier-vocabulary-narrowed, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 asserts a first-class proposal artifact 'is always full or partial, never illustrative', but parent dc-3 ratifies verification coverage as three-valued and disclosed PER ARTIFACT (full/partial/illustrative), and parent dc-1 requires extraction to fail closed to a LOWER verification tier rather than block authoring. Under the parent, a proposal whose mermaid has no generator (a non-flowchart diagram) fails closed to the illustrative tier; the story forbids that state for proposals, leaving such an artifact with no defined coverage tier (and dc-3's Finding text, which discloses only 'full' or 'partial', cannot honestly describe it). The story rationalizes via dc-8's body-figure convention, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-3's three-valued per-artifact vocabulary or dc-1's fail-closed path, and dc-8 as written governs where illustrative diagrams live, not what tier an already-authored first-class proposal falls to when extraction fails. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-illustrative-marker-not-a-field, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 states that 'dc-7's fourth ratified item, the illustrative marker, is deliberately NOT a diagram-artifact field at all' (reading it as dc-8's body-figure document-location convention). Parent dc-7, however, ratifies the illustrative marker as one of four items in a single 02 amendment batch explicitly described as 'the schema surface' for the diagram artifact (alongside status vocabulary, derived_from/base-digest, and scope — all frontmatter fields). If the marker was ratified as part of the artifact schema surface, the story's exclusion of it from the frontmatter enumeration contradicts dc-7 without a declared supersedes/exempts edge; if the story's reading is correct, dc-7's grouping of a body-prose convention into a 'schema surface' batch is itself the decision being silently reinterpreted. Either way the reconciliation is asserted in prose, not declared as an edge. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/alignment-section#dc-1, spec/alignment-section#dc-2, spec/alignment-section#dc-3, spec/alignment-section#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"proposal-tier-vocabulary-narrowed\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 asserts a first-class proposal artifact 'is always full or partial, never illustrative', but parent dc-3 ratifies verification coverage as three-valued and disclosed PER ARTIFACT (full/partial/illustrative), and parent dc-1 requires extraction to fail closed to a LOWER verification tier rather than block authoring. Under the parent, a proposal whose mermaid has no generator (a non-flowchart diagram) fails closed to the illustrative tier; the story forbids that state for proposals, leaving such an artifact with no defined coverage tier (and dc-3's Finding text, which discloses only 'full' or 'partial', cannot honestly describe it). The story rationalizes via dc-8's body-figure convention, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-3's three-valued per-artifact vocabulary or dc-1's fail-closed path, and dc-8 as written governs where illustrative diagrams live, not what tier an already-authored first-class proposal falls to when extraction fails.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"illustrative-marker-not-a-field\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 states that 'dc-7's fourth ratified item, the illustrative marker, is deliberately NOT a diagram-artifact field at all' (reading it as dc-8's body-figure document-location convention). Parent dc-7, however, ratifies the illustrative marker as one of four items in a single 02 amendment batch explicitly described as 'the schema surface' for the diagram artifact (alongside status vocabulary, derived_from/base-digest, and scope — all frontmatter fields). If the marker was ratified as part of the artifact schema surface, the story's exclusion of it from the frontmatter enumeration contradicts dc-7 without a declared supersedes/exempts edge; if the story's reading is correct, dc-7's grouping of a body-prose convention into a 'schema surface' batch is itself the decision being silently reinterpreted. Either way the reconciliation is asserted in prose, not declared as an edge.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-proposal-tier-vocabulary-narrowed** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 asserts a first-class proposal artifact 'is always full or partial, never illustrative', but parent dc-3 ratifies verification coverage as three-valued and disclosed PER ARTIFACT (full/partial/illustrative), and parent dc-1 requires extraction to fail closed to a LOWER verification tier rather than block authoring. Under the parent, a proposal whose mermaid has no generator (a non-flowchart diagram) fails closed to the illustrative tier; the story forbids that state for proposals, leaving such an artifact with no defined coverage tier (and dc-3's Finding text, which discloses only 'full' or 'partial', cannot honestly describe it). The story rationalizes via dc-8's body-figure convention, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-3's three-valued per-artifact vocabulary or dc-1's fail-closed path, and dc-8 as written governs where illustrative diagrams live, not what tier an already-authored first-class proposal falls to when extraction fails. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-illustrative-marker-not-a-field** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 states that 'dc-7's fourth ratified item, the illustrative marker, is deliberately NOT a diagram-artifact field at all' (reading it as dc-8's body-figure document-location convention). Parent dc-7, however, ratifies the illustrative marker as one of four items in a single 02 amendment batch explicitly described as 'the schema surface' for the diagram artifact (alongside status vocabulary, derived_from/base-digest, and scope — all frontmatter fields). If the marker was ratified as part of the artifact schema surface, the story's exclusion of it from the frontmatter enumeration contradicts dc-7 without a declared supersedes/exempts edge; if the story's reading is correct, dc-7's grouping of a body-prose convention into a 'schema surface' batch is itself the decision being silently reinterpreted. Either way the reconciliation is asserted in prose, not declared as an edge. (confidence 0.40)
